2605.22142.title: Short-Term-to-Long-Term Memory Transfer for Knowledge Graphs under Partial Observability
2605.22142.paper_summary: Reinforcement learning under partial observability requires deciding what information to retain, yet most memory-based approaches do not explicitly model short-term-to-long-term transfer of symbolic observations. We study this transfer process in a temporal knowledge-graph memory setting and cast it as a neuro-symbolic value-based decision problem: for each observed triple, the agent chooses whether to keep or drop it before long-term insertion. To handle variable-sized short-term buffers, we use a per-item Q-learning design with shared parameters and a practical temporal-difference update over matched items across consecutive steps. On the RoomKG benchmark at long-term memory capacity 128, learned transfer decisions outperform symbolic and neural baselines, including symbolic baselines with temporal annotations and history-based LSTM/Transformer baselines. Across transfer-policy ablations, a lightweight local short-term-only variant performs best, and step-level behavior shows that the policy keeps navigation- and query-relevant facts while discarding lower-value candidate facts, supporting explicit and interpretable memory decisions under memory constraints.
2605.22142.paper_summary_zh: 強化學習在部分可觀察性下需要決定保留哪些信息，但大多數基於記憶的方法並未明確建模短期到長期的符號觀察轉移。我們在時間知識圖記憶設置中研究這一轉移過程，並將其轉化為一個神經符號價值基決策問題：對於每個觀察到的三元組，代理需要在長期插入之前決定是保留還是丟棄它。為了處理可變大小的短期緩衝區，我們使用每項目 Q 學習設計，並在連續步驟中對匹配項目進行共享參數和實用的時間差更新。在 RoomKG 基準測試中，長期記憶容量為 128，學習的轉移決策超越了符號和神經基準，包括帶有時間註解的符號基準和基於歷史的 LSTM/Transformer 基準。在轉移策略的消融實驗中，一種輕量級的僅短期本地變體表現最佳，步驟級行為顯示該策略保留了導航和查詢相關的事實，同時丟棄了較低價值的候選事實，支持在記憶限制下明確且可解釋的記憶決策。
2605.22142.author: Taewoon Kim et.al.
2605.22142.authors: Taewoon Kim, Vincent François-Lavet, Michael Cochez
